full_prompt: Design a 2-story light wood-frame building module. The module must be 2.44 m x 2.44 m (8 ft x 8 ft) in plan with a story height of 2.44 m (8 ft) per floor for a total height of 4.88 m (16 ft), built entirely from nominal 2x4 (38 mm x 89 mm) Douglas Fir-Larch, Southern Pine, Hem-Fir, or SPF lumber with wood structural panel sheathing on shear walls and horizontal diaphragms. The building must carry simultaneously a floor live load of 1.92 kPa (40 psf) over the full second-floor area, a floor dead load of 0.72 kPa (15 psf), a roof live load of 0.96 kPa (20 psf), a roof dead load of 0.72 kPa (15 psf), and a concentrated lateral racking load of 2.22 kN (500 lbf) applied at the roof level in the weak direction to simulate wind or seismic. Douglas Fir-Larch No.2 members have reference design values per NDS 2024: bending Fb 900 psi (6.2 MPa), tension parallel Ft 575 psi (4.0 MPa), compression parallel Fc 1350 psi (9.3 MPa), modulus of elasticity E 1,600,000 psi (11.0 GPa). All pass/fail criteria must be satisfied: (R1) every stud, plate, joist, rafter, header, and shear wall must achieve a demand-to-capacity ratio at or below 1.0 per NDS 2024 with appropriate adjustment factors, (R2) floor mid-span deflection under 1.0 live plus 1.0 dead must not exceed span/360 which is 6.8 mm, (R3) inter-story drift under the 2.22 kN racking load must not exceed H/400 which is 6.1 mm, (R4) total structural lumber volume in the module must not exceed 0.15 m^3 as an efficiency target. Vibration, thermal, fluid, and radiation analyses are not required. Deliver a STEP AP242 assembly of the full 2-story module plus a cut list tagged with species and grade, and a structural report listing member DCR maxima, mid-floor deflection, roof drift, and total lumber volume.
short_prompt:
Design a 2-story light wood-frame building module 2.44 x 2.44 m (8 ft x 8 ft) in plan with a 2.44 m story height (4.88 m overall), built entirely from nominal 2x4 (38 x 89 mm) Douglas Fir-Larch, Southern Pine, Hem-Fir, or SPF lumber with wood structural panel sheathing on shear walls and horizontal diaphragms. Design per NDS 2024 with appropriate adjustment factors (DCR <= 1.0 on every stud, plate, joist, rafter, header, and shear wall).

Load cases applied simultaneously.
- Floor live 1.92 kPa (40 psf) over the full second-floor area.
- Floor dead 0.72 kPa (15 psf).
- Roof live 0.96 kPa (20 psf).
- Roof dead 0.72 kPa (15 psf).
- Concentrated lateral racking load 2.22 kN (500 lbf) at the roof level in the weak direction (wind/seismic proxy).
Also require floor midspan deflection under 1.0L+1.0D <= span/360 (6.8 mm), inter-story drift under the racking load <= H/400 (6.1 mm), and total structural lumber volume <= 0.15 m^3.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: NDS 2024 member capacity with adjustment factors (CD, CM, Ct, CL, CP, Cfu, Ci, Cr) is a closed-form spreadsheet check on top of the linear FEA demands.

prompt.title: 2-story 8x8 ft light wood-frame module
prompt.brief: Design a 2.44 m x 2.44 m x 4.88 m 2-story light wood-frame module in 2x4 lumber with roof/floor gravity, a 2.22 kN racking load, passing NDS 2024 DCR, deflection, drift, and lumber-volume targets.

objective.primary: pass all NDS 2024 DCR and deflection limits

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: beam for studs/plates/joists, shell for sheathing diaphragms

exclusions_explained.vibration_analysis: Floor vibration checks are not in scope.
exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Lateral load is a prescribed point force.

eval_coverage: NDS 2024 adjustment-factor closed-form check over linear FEA
species/grade library with four legal choices
